core_mechanism: constrained maximum-entropy (mixing-entropy) variational principle that selects the most-probable coarse-grained equilibrium state subject to an infinite hierarchy of conserved quantities (Casimir invariants), yielding a fixed functional relation between the macroscopic field and its conjugate potential
brief_reason: The abstract computes a 'statistical equilibrium' by maximizing a coarse-grained (mixing) entropy under conservation constraints including all Casimir invariants, producing a near-linear functional relation between the macroscopic vorticity and stream function.
